2606.10658.title: Post-Quantum Secure Federated DeFi for Inclusive Banking
2606.10658.paper_summary: Recent advances in error-corrected qubits have accelerated the timeline for practical quantum computing. It poses a threat to cryptographic primitives used to secure financial systems, government infrastructure, communication networks, and DeFi (Decentralized Finance) ecosystems. This paper introduces a post-quantum secure federated DeFi framework that enables inter-bank collaboration to improve the inclusivity of individuals underserved by local lenders due to limited financial histories. Multiple banks contribute encrypted information batches to a virtual server, where lattice-based Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables end-to-end homomorphic computation. The server fuses local data-driven probabilistic assessments, expert beliefs, and verifiable evidence generated by the NASA-IBM Prithvi Geospatial Foundation Model (GFM), in encrypted format. Decentralized technologies are employed to ensure tamper-proof evidence and auditable accountability for all encrypted data exchanges between institutions and the server. The framework is tested on agricultural lending decisions for rural borrowers in Virginia.
2606.10658.paper_summary_zh: 最近在錯誤更正量子位元方面的進展加速了實用量子計算的時間表。這對於用於保護金融系統、政府基礎設施、通信網絡和去中心化金融（DeFi）生態系統的加密原語構成威脅。本文介紹了一個後量子安全的聯邦DeFi框架，該框架使得銀行之間的合作成為可能，以改善因財務歷史有限而未獲得當地貸款機構服務的個體的包容性。多家銀行向虛擬伺服器提供加密信息批次，其中基於格的全同態加密（FHE）實現了端到端的同態計算。伺服器融合了本地數據驅動的概率評估、專家信念和由NASA-IBM Prithvi地理空間基礎模型（GFM）生成的可驗證證據，均以加密格式呈現。採用去中心化技術以確保所有機構與伺服器之間的加密數據交換的防篡改證據和可審計的問責制。該框架在維吉尼亞州的農業貸款決策中進行了測試。
2606.10658.author: Swati Sachan et.al.
2606.10658.authors: Swati Sachan, Dale Fickett, Richard Buchinger, Theo Miller
